_meta.source: Human review of the persisted paratots draft, 2026-06-12. The fact-check thread read the draft and named two defects + two partner-sacred lines that look like defects to a naive critic. This fixture captures the ground truth so every future critique-page SKILL edit is judged against the same known answers.
_meta.doctrine_note: False-positive guard matters as much as catches. A critic that red-flags partner-sacred language burns strategist trust faster than one that misses a claim. The must_not_flag arm of this fixture is the trust guard.

must_flag.summary: The draft section 3 invents a check-in workflow + teacher-walks-to-room procedure; no atom or fact in the project's inventory carries either fact.
must_flag.verbatim_substring: check-in is inside the lobby, and a teacher will walk you to the room
must_flag.rationale: A partner-facing logistic claim that isn't grounded in any source is a content-truthfulness defect. claim_plausibility per the SKILL's axis-1 rubric: 'Every concrete claim traces to atom / fact / stage_1 / merge. No invented programs, no invented people, no invented services.' Procedure section names this exact pattern in its worked example.

must_flag.summary: Section 3's heading + tagline + items repeat the same logistics (Saturday 9:15 AM time, location, or program name) multiple times within the SAME section. Cross-section repetition is fine; intra-section is a coherence defect.
must_flag.rationale: The voice_character procedure under 'Procedural decomposition — intra-section coherence' calls this out exactly. Even if voice is otherwise clean, redundancy within one section = ≤80 score per the rubric.

must_not_flag.summary: The lowercase 'before you were even born, god loved you first' is a VERBATIM lift from a prose_snippet atom (94df26ac-175e-45d7-b447-29a2ebbd91d2). The lowercase 'god' is preserved per the partner's intentional casing.
must_not_flag.verbatim_substring: before you were even born, god loved you first
must_not_flag.rationale: A naive critic would flag the lowercase 'god' as a capitalization error. It is the partner's INTENTIONAL writing, preserved verbatim per the SKILL's hard rule on verbatim atoms. Flagging this would burn strategist trust faster than missing a real claim.

must_not_flag.summary: 'Generous, Inclusive, Affirming Love' is a triad heading that LOOKS like the filler-triad pattern audit-criteria flags. It is in fact a verbatim lift from voice_sample atom 73a9658c — the partner's intentional theological language naming three distinct attributes of divine love.
must_not_flag.rationale: Naive triad-pattern matching would flag this. The audit-criteria 'Filler adjective triads' rule explicitly says distinguish filler from intentional ('safe, known, and loved' — distinct semantic weight). This triad lifts from a voice_sample atom — three distinct theological propositions, partner-sacred. The first critique fire DID flag this and then defended it (nit/voice_character that argues it passes); the correct behavior is to NOT flag it at all.
